title: Why You Can't Trust Your Own Memories

segments.text: Your memories might not be as real as you think.
Memory is a reconstructive process, not a playback.
Every time you recall a memory, you alter it.
Emotions can distort your recollection significantly.
Even details fade and change over time.
So, can you truly trust what you remember?
Keep this in mind when reminiscing.
Follow Curio for your daily dose of science.

description: Your memories might be unreliable! Follow for daily science & tech!🧠
